title: 山核桃大街谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\121-山核桃大街谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
In Mrs. Haberdashere's sitting room, Inspector Sharp discusses the investigation into Valerie Hobhouse with Hercule Poirot. Sharp explains that Hobhouse used Celia Austin’s kleptomania as a ruse to facilitate a sophisticated smuggling operation involving drugs and gems. While Sharp notes that they lack concrete evidence to charge Hobhouse with the murder of Celia, Poirot reveals a different truth regarding the death of Patricia Lane.

Poirot explains that the phone call made to the police station at 6:08, which appeared to be from Patricia Lane, was actually made by Valerie Hobhouse from a pharmacy telephone booth to create an alibi. He asserts that Nigel Chapman is the true murderer of Patricia Lane. Poirot argues that Chapman, who possessed morphine and exhibited reckless, overconfident behavior, killed Patricia by hitting her from behind. He notes that Chapman was able to "recognize" the fake call because he was the one who answered it immediately after committing the crime. Chapman had attempted to stage a double bluff using green ink and planted Lennie Bates's hair in Patricia's fingernails, but Poirot views these as the mistakes of a self-centered man.

Following this, Poirot meets with Mr. Endicott, a lawyer, to discuss the recent death of Arthur Stanley. Poirot inquires about the death of Arthur's wife several years prior, which was ruled an accidental barbiturate overdose. Endicott notes that Arthur provided evidence regarding his wife's confusion. Poirot concludes the meeting by asking Endicott's opinion on whether Arthur Stanley was the type of man who would kill his wife to marry another woman.
